welcome.label: // example-project
welcome.title:
Explore a realistic workspace
with the plugin installed.
welcome.subtitle: This repo now simulates a small TypeScript application. The project code lives in src/index.ts, project rules live in CLAUDE.md and .claude/settings.json, and the reusable plugin is installed at .claude/plugins/vision-plugin/.
welcome.secondary: Open the project root first, then inspect the installed plugin to see what stays project-specific and what can be shared across repositories.

claude-md.title: Project Memory
claude-md.description: Application-specific context that explains the project, architecture, and installed plugin location.

settings.title: Project Settings
settings.description: How Claude is configured locally and how the installed plugin is discovered during development.

plugins.title: Installed Plugin
plugins.description: The Hello Pomelo plugin as it appears inside a real workspace under .claude/plugins/vision-plugin.

agents.title: Installed Agents
agents.description: Review subagents shipped by the plugin once it is present in the project workspace.

skills.title: Installed Skills
skills.description: Reusable frontend, backend, testing, security, and deployment knowledge loaded from the installed plugin.

hooks.title: Plugin Hooks
hooks.description: Hook config and shell guards bundled with the plugin and available from the project workspace.

mcp.title: Project MCP
mcp.description: External context configured at the project root alongside the installed plugin.

code.title: Workspace Architecture
code.description: A minimal TypeScript entrypoint and package scripts that live next to the installed plugin.

children.name: settings.json
children.label: Project Settings
children.description: Claude settings for the local workspace, including how the installed plugin is used during development.

children.name: PLUGINS.md
children.label: Installed Plugin Layout
children.description: Explains why the plugin sits under .claude/plugins inside the workspace and what remains project-specific.

children.name: plugin.json
children.label: Installed Plugin Manifest
children.description: The manifest that Claude discovers inside the installed plugin directory.

children.name: README.md
children.label: Plugin README
children.description: The reusable plugin package documentation, now shown from the perspective of a consuming project.

children.name: pr-orchestrator.md
children.label: PR Orchestrator
children.description: Lead reviewer that classifies a diff and delegates to specialist agents.

children.name: code-reviewer.md
children.label: Code Reviewer
children.description: Checks bugs, architecture fit, conventions, and risky implementation details.

children.name: security-reviewer.md
children.label: Security Reviewer
children.description: Focuses on RBAC, secrets, uploads, webhooks, and API route hardening.

children.name: performance-reviewer.md
children.label: Performance Reviewer
children.description: Looks for bundle regressions, data-loading waste, and expensive rendering patterns.

children.name: test-coverage.md
children.label: Test Coverage Reviewer
children.description: Flags missing unit, integration, and E2E coverage in changed areas.

children.name: architect.md
children.label: Architect
children.description: Helps design PIM, DAM, ERP, and e-commerce application architecture with explicit trade-offs.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Frontend Patterns
children.description: Patterns for Next.js admin interfaces, tables, dashboards, forms, and performance.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Backend Patterns
children.description: Guidance for services, repositories, auth, queues, and structured logging.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Security Review
children.description: OWASP-style checks adapted for RBAC-heavy business applications.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: E2E Testing
children.description: Playwright guidance for multi-step workflows, RBAC, and import or export flows.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Deployment Patterns
children.description: Next.js deployment rules for Docker, CI, health checks, and rollback.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: API Design
children.description: Resource naming, pagination, filtering, versioning, and error response conventions.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Database Patterns
children.description: Safe migrations, indexing, RLS, and query design for data-heavy catalogs.

children.name: SKILL.md
children.label: Git Workflow
children.description: Branch naming, protected branch policy, and PR rules for the plugin rollout.

children.name: hooks.json
children.label: Plugin Hook Configuration
children.description: Hook definitions shipped by the installed plugin.

children.name: branch-guard.sh
children.label: Branch Guard Script
children.description: Shell guard that denies dangerous git pushes from protected branches.

children.name: index.ts
children.label: Workspace Entry Point
children.description: A minimal source file that shows where normal project code lives once the plugin is installed.

terminal.welcomeTagline: Trace the workspace from .claude and src/ into the installed plugin at .claude/plugins/vision-plugin.

quickStart.description: verify the installed plugin and source layout
see how Claude would inspect project changes

howToExplore: Open .claude/ to see the project-level Claude configuration and installed plugin
Open src/ to see the minimal application code that stays outside the plugin
Read CLAUDE.md for the project-specific rules and directory map
Dive into .claude/plugins/vision-plugin/ to inspect the installed plugin files

doctorChecks.label: CLAUDE.md
doctorChecks.detail: workspace memory loaded from project root

doctorChecks.label: Review agents
doctorChecks.detail: 6 agent files discovered inside the plugin

doctorChecks.label: Skills
doctorChecks.detail: 8 skills available from the installed plugin

doctorChecks.label: Source tree
doctorChecks.detail: Minimal TypeScript entrypoint found at src/index.ts

initConventions: Project CLAUDE.md and .mcp.json stay at the workspace root
memoryEntries: The installed plugin lives under .claude/plugins/vision-plugin during local development
Project code stays in src/, even when the workspace only has a single index.ts entrypoint
Plugin hooks protect git workflow while project rules remain in CLAUDE.md
.mcp.json belongs at the workspace root because MCP servers are configured per project
The plugin can recommend external context sources without owning the project root config
